records.ety: [L., a rain storm, a rain cloud, the cloudshaped which enveloped the gods when they appeared on earth.]

defs: A circle, or disk, or any indication of radiant light around the heads of divinities, saints, and sovereigns, upon medals, pictures, etc.; a halo. See Aureola, and Glory, n., 5.
notes: ☞ “The nimbus is of pagan origin.” “As an attribute of power, the nimbus is often seen attached to the heads of evil spirits.” Fairholl.

defs: A rain cloud; one of the four principal varieties of clouds. See Cloud.
